text: Once the pericardium is opened, a pericardial window is created in order to expose the free LV wall.
The ideal placement for the lead is as close to the base of the heart as possible.
Left atrial appendage should be seen through the window.
It's also important to avoid any of the coronary arteries or veins, but also to have a good portion of muscle to aim the LV lead.
The LV lead is then pushed through the 15mm port and is going to be screwed into the free LV wall.
Once tested, the rest of the LV lead is inserted into the chest cavity and can be pulled out through the pacemaker pocket.
Chest tube is then placed, usually a silicone drain or a Blake drain.
